description: Test your recall of key vitamin facts, deficiency diseases, and biochemical functions.

cards.text: What are the four fat-soluble vitamins?
cards.answer: A, D, E, K/Vitamins A, D, E, and K

tip.tip: They dissolve in lipids and require bile for absorption

cards.text: What is the chromophore in rhodopsin needed for dim-light vision?

tip.tip: It is the aldehyde form of Vitamin A

cards.text: What are Bitot's spots and which vitamin deficiency causes them?
cards.answer: Foamy white patches on the conjunctiva caused by Vitamin A deficiency/Vitamin A

tip.tip: A classic eye sign seen in malnourished children in India

cards.text: Why are 70-90% of Indians deficient in Vitamin D?
cards.answer: Limited sun exposure and dietary factors/lack of sunlight exposure

tip.tip: Think about lifestyle practices and skin coverage

cards.text: What is the provitamin form of Vitamin A found in orange and yellow vegetables?

tip.tip: Carrots, papaya, and mango are rich sources

cards.text: Which vitamin deficiency causes pellagra, and in which Indian states is it still seen?
cards.answer: Niacin (Vitamin B3) deficiency; Maharashtra and Karnataka/niacin

tip.tip: It occurs in sorghum-belt areas where the staple diet lacks this B vitamin

cards.text: Why can fat-soluble vitamins cause toxicity (hypervitaminosis) but water-soluble vitamins usually cannot?
cards.answer: Fat-soluble vitamins are stored in liver and adipose tissue and not readily excreted, while water-soluble vitamins are excreted in urine

cards.text: Name the enzyme that cleaves beta-carotene into retinal in the intestinal mucosa.

tip.tip: It produces two molecules of retinal from one beta-carotene

cards.text: Which conditions impair absorption of fat-soluble vitamins?
cards.answer: Fat malabsorption conditions: coeliac disease, cystic fibrosis, obstructive jaundice

tip.tip: Any condition that reduces bile or fat absorption

cards.text: What is the only water-soluble vitamin with significant body stores lasting years?

tip.tip: It is stored in the liver for 3-5 years
